description: Following the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster in March 2011, the German government under Angela Merkel reversed its previous policy to extend the lifespans of nuclear power plants. This led to a massive shift in public support for the 'Energiewende' (Energy Turnaround) policy, mandating a transition to renewables and a total nuclear phaseout by 2022.

key_events.description: Fukushima Daiichi nuclear accident occurs, triggering immediate global scrutiny.
Merkel announces a 3-month moratorium on nuclear plant life extensions.
Hundreds of thousands participate in massive anti-nuclear demonstrations across Germany.
Bundestag formally votes to shut down all nuclear reactors by 2022.

calibration_notes: Ideal for measuring 'shock-event' volatility and how a singular global crisis can permanently shift national policy paradigms.
